what_it_does: A collective, provisional design brief / interpretive ledger for the company's front interface. It records Tim's directive, extracts requirements, adds empirical direction data from real snapshots, offers a lead synthesis, and collects per-member contributions toward a buildable 'overlord' interface.
observations: Tim's transcribed message is preserved verbatim and framed as the primary input.
The interface is declared a bootstrap, not a late feature, meant to end Tim-as-bottleneck.
An empirical section mines 7,141 snapshots (460 real annotations) and asserts direction is always element-bound.
A minimum direction vocabulary is derived from counts: typed-comment, reaction-stamp, favour, highlight.
The lead convergence proposes composing projection's Instrument, wildcard's element-direction, and fork's loadable-brain over the address spine.
Member sections from fork, projection, and wildcard each list what already exists and where their seams meet.
Tri-form-factor native layouts (desktop, mobile portrait, mobile landscape) are claimed already built for the Instrument.
The address system is described as 16 schemes, 9 resolvable through one resolver.
A compatibility flag notes the lead mis-stated the click-to-talk wire; two distinct mechanisms exist.
A build-hold directive is recorded: design only, no build on assumptions.
standouts: All real direction is element-bound; there is no slide-level-only direction in the data.
The interface should extend projection's existing Instrument rather than start fresh.
Scale-axis position is proposed to select both the brain (FOCUS vs PANEL) and the projection mode (graph vs slide).
Three candidate 'loadable brain' models are flagged but not decided.
A concrete capture grammar {element_id, annotation_type, text, reaction, favour} is offered as the reusable write-back contract.
conventions: Uses wiki-style bracket links such as [[feedback-confident-not-correct]] and [[the-four-root-axes]].
Sections are prefixed by member/channel identifiers.
Starred (★) markers highlight key claims, corrections, and unresolved issues.
Tim's words are quoted faithfully with only light cleanup.
Empirical claims include counts and explicit verification instructions.
concerns: Several unresolved forks require a Tim/fabric decision (brain model, D1 graph-of-minds, state-axis formalization).
Some claims are 'built-unverified' while others are 'verified-by-use' — confidence levels are mixed.
The lead convergence and member contributions overlap; full reconciliation is not complete.
The file is long and contains nested corrections, which may obscure the current agreed position.
The instruction to ignore most existing UIs while reusing the substrate under them could be ambiguous in practice.
notes: A dense, multi-voice design brief that tries to turn Tim's 'overlord interface' into a composable build plan by fusing an existing projection wheel, address spine, element-bound direction grammar, and a loadable Claude-Code brain.
questions: Which of the three candidate loadable-brain models will be selected?
What exactly is the 'different area' Tim wants projection's work extended into?
How does DNA's root-3 frame/identity concretely shape the rendered surface?
What is the precise formalization of the state/phase axis and verification-state?
How will the 24K-line throwaway UI be separated from the reusable substrate in practice?
What is the first concrete build slice once the design hold is lifted?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches its stated purpose as a brief, but it also pre-converges an architecture and parks several unresolved build decisions rather than remaining purely open-ended.
apparent_themes: Empirical grounding in real snapshot data
intention_signals: Get Tim out of Claude Code as the bottleneck by building a principled front interface now, reusing already-built pieces, while surfacing unresolved decisions for Tim and the fabric to steer.
novelty: Treats the interface as a projection of an addressed state; uses mined annotation statistics to define the minimum interaction vocabulary; proposes that the scale axis selects both the brain and the projection mode; frames the loadable brain as a conversation bound to an element's address.
contribution: A shared reference capturing Tim's requirements, an empirical direction vocabulary, a candidate composable architecture, per-member perspectives and seams, and explicit unresolved decision flags for downstream steering.
summary_for_embedding: This file is a collective, provisional design brief for an 'overlord' front interface that synthesizes Tim's directive, empirical annotation data, and member contributions into a composable architecture built atop an address spine, projection wheel, element-bound direction grammar, and loadable Claude-Code brain.
intention_for_embedding: It was written to align the fabric on what to build now to unblock Tim's oversight and to surface unresolved design decisions for Tim to steer.
